- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.

1	# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code Session Guide
2	
3	Always read this file and `AGENTS.md`. Everything else is on-demand — the table below is your map.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Memory
8	
9	User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
10	
11	Active branch: `sandbox-albert`. Never merge or switch branches. All commits go to `sandbox-albert` only.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Document map — what to load and when
16	
17	| Document | What it covers | Load it when… |
18	|---|---|---|
19	| `AGENTS.md` | Authoritative dev guide: decision tree, file nav, idiosyncratic decisions, data model, incident log | **Always** — read this every session |
20	| `docs/rfq-math.md` | Every pricing formula: INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, Scenario 1/2/3, royalty rules | Working on calculations, `equation.ts`, margin/sell-price logic |
21	| `docs/architecture.md` | Module structure, AG Grid patterns, auth flow, data flow diagrams | Module structure is unclear, or adding a new feature area |
22	| `src/app/pages/rfq/README.md` | RFQ file map, recalculation trigger chain, AG Grid patterns specific to RFQ | Working inside the `rfq/` module |
23	| `docs/development.md` | Local setup, test, lint, debug playbook | Setting up locally, running tests, debugging a specific issue type |
24	| `docs/configuration.md` | Environment files, build configs, Cloud Build variables | Adding/changing env vars or build targets |
25	| `docs/deployment.md` | Cloud Build → Docker → Cloud Run pipeline, rollback | Touching `cloudbuild.yaml`, `Dockerfile`, or `nginx.conf` |
26	| `HANDOFF.md` | Session handoff notes — temporary, delete when items are resolved | Picking up work from a prior session |
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Context management
31	
32	Large files that frequently re-enter context:
33	- `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.ts` (~3600 lines)
34	- `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts` (~600 lines)
35	- `src/app/pages/rfq/detail-cell-renderer/detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` (~1300 lines)
36	
37	When working in these files, read only the relevant section (use `offset` + `limit`). Do not read the entire file unless doing a full audit.
38	
39	---
40	
41	## .claudeignore guidance
42	
43	See `.claudeignore` in the repo root. The file excludes `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `src/assets/`, and `src/static-data/` from indexing. Add any large generated files that appear during development.
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Operations permissions
48	
49	- **Allowed:** Read, edit, write files in `src/`, `docs/`, root config files
50	- **Allowed:** `git add`, `git commit`, `git push origin sandbox-albert`
51	- **Allowed:** `node`, `yarn`, `ng` CLI commands
52	- **Not allowed:** `git merge`, `git checkout <branch>`, `git push --force`
53	- **Not allowed:** Editing files in `node_modules/` or `dist/`
54	
55	## Running tests locally
56	
57	`node_modules/` is installed at `C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-frontend\node_modules\`. Run the full suite before every commit:
58	
59	```powershell
60	# Full suite (same command CI runs)
61	yarn test
62	
63	# Single spec file
64	yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"
65	
66	# Watch mode during active development
67	yarn test:watch
68	```
69	
70	`yarn test` completes in ~12 s locally. Always run it before pushing — CI takes ~3 min per attempt.
71	
72	---
73	
74	## Pre-commit checklist — colDef / spec changes
75	
76	Run `yarn test` locally before every commit. A failing test = failed CI build = wasted time. Additional checks when touching colDef config files or spec files:
77	
78	1. **commonDefaults is the fallback.** The final colDef shape from `addColumn()` is `{ ...commonDefaults, ...overrides }`. If you remove a property from an override, the value comes from `commonDefaults`, not `undefined`. Read `commonDefaults` before writing any spec assertion.
79	2. **Grep specs for the property name.** Run `Grep pattern="<propertyName>" glob="**/*.spec.ts"` for every property you add, remove, or rename. Confirm each assertion still matches the actual runtime value.
80	3. **No `require()` in spec files.** All spec files use ES module imports. Use the already-imported `readFileSync`, `join`, etc. — never `require('fs')` inline.
81	4. **Trace the full merged shape.** `addColumn()` produces `{ ...commonDefaults, icons, ...overrides, context: { roles } }`. Never assume a property is absent from a column without reading both `commonDefaults` and the override.
82	
83	See AGENTS.md §14 incident log for the full history of why these rules exist.
84	
85	---
86	
87	## Commit style
88	
89	```
90	type(scope): short description
91	
92	Body if needed.
93	
94	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
95	```
96	
97	Types: `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`. Scope examples: `rfq`, `auth`, `equation`, `grid`.
98	
99	Keep messages specific — include commit SHA of related prior commits when relevant.
100	
101	---
102	
103	## AG Grid MCP server — mandatory for all AG Grid work
104	
105	The `ag-grid` MCP server is installed and must be used any time you touch AG Grid code. This prevents referencing removed or deprecated APIs.
106	
107	**On every session start, run:**
108	```
109	mcp__ag-grid__set_versions(version: "35.1.0", framework: "angular")
110	```
111	Note: `detect_version` fails because the project path contains a space (`dflow plm`). Always use `set_versions` directly. The docs server tops out at `35.1.0`; our project is `35.2.1` — patch releases don't change APIs so `35.1.0` docs are accurate.
112	
113	**Before writing any AG Grid code, search the docs first:**
114	```
115	mcp__ag-grid__search_docs(query: "your question here")
116	```
117	Examples:
118	- `"valueSetter async return value"`
119	- `"cellDataType column type inference"`
120	- `"deprecated APIs removed in v35"`
121	- `"getColDef vs colDef"`
122	- `"aggFunc group rows params.data"`
123	
124	**Rule:** Never write AG Grid API calls from memory alone. Always confirm against the MCP docs first — that is how the `userProvidedColDef`, `columnApi`, and `getRowNodeId` bugs were introduced.
125	
126	---
127	
128	## Tool preferences
129	
130	- Use `Read` with `offset`/`limit` for large files — never read 3000-line files in full unless auditing
131	- Use `Grep` to find symbols before reading — do not read files blindly
132	- Use `Agent` with `subagent_type: "Explore"` for codebase-wide searches
133	- Prefer `Edit` over `Write` for modifying existing files
134	- Run `git diff HEAD` before committing to verify the diff is what you expect
135	
136	---
137	
138	## Angular Material `mat-select` rules — mandatory
139	
140	Violations here have cost multiple sessions and 5+ wasted commits. Read before touching any `mat-select`.
141	
142	1. **`mat-select` silently does nothing on click → check `options.length` first.** `MatSelect._canOpen()` requires `options?.length > 0`. Zero `mat-option` children = panel never opens, no error, no warning. Count `mat-option` children in DevTools before assuming a bug elsewhere.
143	
144	2. **Options must come from a synchronous array, not `| async`.** `MatSelect` uses `@ContentChildren(MatOption)` to find its options. Options rendered asynchronously via `@for (x of obs | async)` can be missed. Always iterate over a plain component array (e.g. `license: any[]`) populated directly. See how `divisionCodeList` is used for `Division` — that's the correct pattern.
145	
146	3. **Do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]` on the same `mat-select`.** They are from two different forms APIs and both try to own the value. Use `[formControl]` alone for reactive forms.
147	
148	4. **Never add `(click)="select.open()"` to a `mat-select` host.** It creates a re-open loop: CDK backdrop fires close, click bubbles and re-opens. The user cannot dismiss the panel. Call `select.open()` programmatically after data loads instead.
149	
150	5. **Disable a `mat-select` FormControl when its options are not yet loaded.** A select with zero options silently refuses to open. Use `control.disable()` while data is loading and `control.enable()` after, so the user sees a greyed-out field instead of a silent no-op.
151	
152	6. **Never give a template ref (`#name`) the same name as a component property.** The template ref shadows the property in all template expressions. `[(ngModel)]="chatInput"` will bind to the HTMLInputElement, not the string, producing `"[object HTMLInputElement]"`. Use native `[value]` + `(input)` binding as the immune alternative.
153	
154	See AGENTS.md §14 for the full incident history behind these rules.
155	
156	---
157	
158	## Claude-specific behaviors
159	
160	- When a calculation is changed, always state the formula in the commit message body (e.g., `LDP = (FOB * (1 + agent/100)) * (1 + duty/100) + duty_dollar + freight`)
161	- When fixing an AG Grid deprecated API, cite the AG Grid version it was removed in
162	- Do not use `window['anything']` in new code — use proper Angular DI
163	- Do not use `.toPromise()` — always use `firstValueFrom()` from `rxjs`
164	- Do not add `aggFunc` to any RFQ grid column without reading AGENTS.md §11 first

content.content: 1 # AGENTS.md — Designflow Frontend: Primary Developer Guide 2 3 Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository. 4 5 --- 6 7 ## 1. What this project is 8 9 Designflow is a product-lifecycle-management (PLM) web application used by sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams to manage the full journey of a product from concept to retail. The core workflows are: **RFQ** (request for quote — collect vendor pricing and compute landed-cost and margin chains), **Item Library** (product master data), **Standardized Products**, **Art Piece** management, and **Production/Licensing Tracking**. This repository is the Angular frontend only; the backend REST API is a separate service at `api.designflow.app`. The business-critical outcome is correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid — errors there directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes. 10 11 --- 12 13 ## 2. 1	# Architecture
2	
3	## System overview
4	
5	Designflow Frontend is a pure Angular SPA — no server-side rendering. It communicates with a separate backend REST API over HTTPS. The frontend is responsible for all UI, routing, role enforcement, and business-logic calculations (margin chains, landed cost). The backend handles persistence and data retrieval.
6	
7	```
8	Browser
9	  └── Angular SPA (this repo)
10	        ├── AG Grid Enterprise (primary data display and editing)
11	        ├── Angular Material (forms, dialogs, snackbars)
12	        ├── Auth (JWT in localStorage, idle timeout)
13	        └── HTTP client
14	              └── Backend REST API (separate service)
15	                    ├── /api/core        ← main CRUD
16	                    ├── /api/tracking    ← production/licensing tracking
17	                    ├── /api/item_master ← item library
18	                    └── /api/data_sync   ← sync operations
19	```

82	## Authentication and authorization
83	
84	Two login paths both produce the same Designflow RS256 JWT and follow the same post-login flow:
85	
86	**Password login:**
87	1. User submits email + password → `POST /auth/login` → backend returns JWT
88	2. `auth.service.ts` stores token in `localStorage`
89	
90	**Microsoft SSO (Azure AD):**
91	1. `AzureSsoService.initialize()` runs at app start via `APP_INITIALIZER` — creates the MSAL `PublicClientApplication` instance (MSAL Browser 5.x)
92	2. User clicks "Sign in with Microsoft" → `AzureSsoService.loginPopup()` opens a Microsoft popup with scopes `['openid', 'profile', 'email']`; the popup redirects to `/assets/auth-redirect.html` (the MSAL redirect bridge page), which fires a `BroadcastChannel` message back to the parent window and closes
93	3. MSAL returns the Azure AD ID token → `POST /auth/azure` → backend verifies the token via Azure JWKS, looks up or auto-provisions the user, returns a Designflow JWT
94	4. `auth.service.ts` stores the returned Designflow JWT in `localStorage`
95	
96	**Common post-login flow:**
97	1. `UserAuthInterceptor` injects `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every outgoing request
98	2. `AuthGuardService.canActivate()` calls `auth.service.isAuthenticated()` → verifies token with backend + checks JWT expiry
99	3. `RoleGuardService.canActivate()` decodes the JWT payload, compares `role` to `route.data.role`
100	4. `IdleTimeoutService` watches for inactivity and logs the user out
101	
102	**Role values (from JWT payload):** `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`
103	
104	**Auto-provisioning:** First-time Azure SSO users are created in the `users` table with `level: 'designer'`. An admin promotes them via User Management.
105	
106	**Single-use token:** `/singleUseToken/:token/:rfqItem_priceCode/:highlight` — allows vendors to access a specific RFQ item without a full account.
107	
108	**Token storage:** `localStorage` (not a cookie). Tokens survive page refresh and are cleared on `localStorage.clear()`.
109	
110	**MSAL redirect bridge:** MSAL Browser 5.x changed the popup flow to require a dedicated blank page at the redirect URI instead of the full SPA. `/assets/auth-redirect.html` loads `msal-redirect-bridge.min.js` (copied from `node_modules/@azure/msal-browser/lib/redirect-bridge` at build time) and calls `broadcastResponseToMainFrame()`. Without this page, the popup would load the full Angular app and never relay the auth code back to the parent window.
111

114	## HTTP interceptor stack
115	
116	Interceptors run in declaration order (from `app.module.ts`):
117	
118	1. **`UserAuthInterceptor`** — attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header from localStorage
119	2. **`LoaderInterceptor`** — shows/hides global spinner for requests that include `X-Enable-Loader: true`; call `this.setUserAuthWithLoader()` instead of `this.setUserAuth()` in `main.service.ts` to opt in
120	3. **`HttpErrorInterceptor`** — catches HTTP errors, extracts a user-facing message via `src/app/helpers/api-error.ts`, displays it in a snackbar

123	
124	## AG Grid patterns
125	
126	All feature grids use AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1 with the client-side row model (CSRM).
127	
128	### Column identity
129	
130	Each column has two identifiers:
131	- **`field`** — the key used to read/write `params.data[field]`. Can contain spaces and special characters (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`).
132	- **`colId`** — the stable lowercase-underscore identifier used in code (e.g. `"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`).
133	
134	`getMasterFiledName(colId)` (in both `rfq.component.ts` and `equation.ts`) resolves a colId to its field name by iterating `this.colHeaders`. **Always use this function** — never hardcode a field name string in calculation code.
135	
136	### Master-detail grids
137	
138	RFQ and Production Tracking use AG Grid master-detail. The master row is an RFQ item; the detail row is vendor quotes. The `DetailCellRenderer` component manages the detail grid independently, with its own column definitions loaded from the backend.
139	
140	### Row grouping
141	
142	The RFQ grid groups by `RFQ Group` (column with `rowGroup: true`, `enableRowGroup: true`). Group rows do not have `params.data` in AG Grid's aggregation pipeline. **Do not add `aggFunc` to any column** — see AGENTS.md §11 for the full explanation of why.
143	
144	### `postSortRows`
145	
146	Used on the RFQ grid to keep group rows ordered by Date Modified without activating the aggregation pipeline.
147	
148	### Async valueSetters
149	
150	AG Grid v35 awaits an `async valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise cancels the edit and reverts the cell. Every async valueSetter must:
151	- Return `true` on success
152	- Return `false` (or call `_snackBar.open(...)`) on validation failure
153	- Never let an uncaught exception propagate (wrap in try-catch)
154	
155	### View layout persistence
156	
157	The RFQ grid supports named views (column visibility, filter state, column order) saved per user via `POST /saveGridLayout` / `POST /getCustomGridLayout`. The `layout-dropdown` component manages this.
158

161	## RFQ calculation pipeline
162	
163	See [`src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`](../src/app/pages/rfq/README.md) for the full formula reference.
164	
165	**Summary:** When a user edits a sourcing field (FOB Cost, CBM/pc, agent commission, duty rate, freight, container cost), the valueSetter chain is:
166	
167	```
168	User edits cell
169	  → valueSetter in rfq.component.ts
170	      → calculateLandedCost() (if relevant field changed)
171	          → updates landed_cost field on params.data
172	      → equation.recalculate_Lic_Gen(params, colHeaders)
173	          → setGen{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()  ×4
174	          → setLic{Fob,Mddp,Poe,Whse}Margin()  ×4
175	              → each setter computes sell price from margin, or margin from sell price
176	  → return true (commit the edit)
177	```
178

183	```
184	ngOnInit
185	  → getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq' })    ← column definitions from backend
186	      → applyAutoFormatters()             ← apply date formatters, comparators
187	      → applyColumnDefinitions()          ← wire valueSetters, valueGetters per colId
188	  → getAllItem({ status, divisionCode })  ← row data
189	      → gridApi.setRowData()
190	  → getCustomGridLayout()                 ← restore saved column/filter state
191	```

209	## AI classification + duty-rate breakdown subsystem
210	
211	The duty-rate dialog in the RFQ grid uses an AI model only for HTS classification — every rate component comes from an authoritative lookup. The dialog presents the rate as a three-field breakdown (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and stores it on the row as an equation alongside the evaluated total.
212	
213	```
214	Editor (admin / sourcing_manager)
215	  ├── AI Settings        Model picker (GET /api/openrouter/models, PUT /api/admin/app-settings/ai-model)
216	  │                      Section 301 PDF upload (POST /api/admin/section301/upload)
217	  ├── Trump 2025 Tariffs Per-country reciprocal-rate table (GET/PUT /api/admin/trump2025/list)
218	  └── Factory Countries  Per-factory factory_country editor (GET /api/admin/factories, PATCH /api/admin/factories/:id)
219	
220	RFQ duty-rate dialog (sourcing_manager, admin)
221	  ├── desc cleaner       Strips brand/size/color (src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/desc-cleaner.ts)
222	  ├── CROSS search       GET /api/cross/search?term= → CBP binding rulings
223	  ├── AI chat            POST /api/openrouter/chat → classification JSON only (no rate)
224	  ├── MFN lookup         GET /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=    → bundled USITC schedule
225	  ├── Section 301 lookup GET /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=   → list + rate from USTR PDF
226	  └── Trump 2025 lookup  GET /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country={fobCountry}
227	
228	RFQ sub-grid (per-quote)
229	  └── FOB Country auto-fill on Vendor pick (computeFobAutofill helper)
230	      ← GET /api/admin/factories/countries → { factory_nickname: factory_country }
231	```
232	
233	**Key services:**
234	- `AiSettingsService` — every admin API in this subsystem (OpenRouter models, Section 301, HTS rates, Trump 2025, Factories).
235	- `HtsClassifierService` — system prompt, classification chat helpers, response parsing.
236	- `CrossService` — CBP CROSS proxy.
237	
238	**Intentional design decisions:**
239	- **AI does not return rates.** The system prompt instructs the model to always return `suggested_duty_pct: null`. The MFN base rate comes from a bundled USITC HTS JSON; Section 301 from the uploaded USTR PDF; Trump 2025 from the admin list. The model picks the HTS code; the rate lookups handle the numbers.
240	- **Section 301 PDF is parsed with pure regex** (no AI). Rates hardcoded per list because they're statutory and don't appear reliably in the PDF text. `pdf-parse` is lazy-required in the backend so a broken package can't crash startup.
241	- **MFN rates are bundled, not live.** `data/hts-rates.json` is generated once a year by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` from `hts.usitc.gov`. O(1) lookup with 10→8→6-digit prefix fallback because the AI usually returns a 10-digit code while the rate sits on the 8-digit parent.
242	- **Trump 2025 rates are in `app_settings`, not bundled.** They change on political timelines, so admins maintain them through the editor UI. Lookup uses an alias table (`"PRC"`/`"Mainland China"`/`"Peoples Republic of China"` → `"China"`) so admins can use the canonical name without breaking incoming FOB-country strings.
243	- **Duty rate is stored as both an equation and an evaluated total.** The cell displays `40.3%`; on edit, `mathExprCellEditor` restores the `"5.3+25+10"` equation. Persisted as `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` (DB) + ephemeral `[colField]_equation` (editor slot). See AGENTS.md §11 for the dual-storage rationale.
244	- **FOB Country auto-fill never overwrites a user value.** The sub-grid renderer pre-fills `FOB Country` from the chosen factory's `factory_country` *only* when the cell is currently empty. The rule is a pure function (`fob-country-autofill.ts`) so it can be unit-tested without AG Grid.
245	- **`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`** is managed via GCP Secret Manager and injected via `--set-secrets` in `cloudbuild.yaml`. Not in `--set-env-vars` (which is a full replacement on each deploy).
246	
247	See [`docs/ai-classification.md`](ai-classification.md) for the full architecture including the multi-model routing pattern and prompt caching strategy.
248	
249	---
250	
251	## Notification system
252	
253	In-app notifications are stored in the backend `user_notification` table and surfaced through a polling-based architecture.
254	
255	**`NotificationService`** (`src/app/helpers/services/notification.service.ts`):
256	- Polls `GET /api/notifications/count` on a 30-second `interval()` Observable
257	- Exposes `unreadCount$` as a `BehaviorSubject<number>` consumed by the toolbar badge
258	- `startPolling()` / `stopPolling()` — called from `ToolbarComponent.ngOnInit()`
259	- CRUD wrappers: `markAsRead(id)`, `markAllAsRead()`, `getMentionableUsers()`, `createNotification(payload)`
260	
261	**Toolbar badge:** The bell icon shows a red pill (capped at `99+`) when `unreadCount$ > 0`.
262	
263	**Notifications page** (`src/app/pages/notifications/`): Two tabs — **Alerts** (rows from `user_notification` with mark-as-read and mark-all buttons) and **Licensing** (existing licensing notifications). Navigated to from the toolbar bell click.
264	
265	**@mention autocomplete** (`src/app/pages/comments/mention-picker/`): The comment form parses `@` keystrokes and opens a user picker populated from `GET /api/notifications/mentionable-users`. Selecting a user inserts `@FirstName` into the comment text. On save, the backend (@mention pipeline in `helpers/mention.js`) triggers notifications for all mentioned users automatically.
266	
267	---
268	
269	## Admin impersonation
270	
271	Admins can view the app exactly as any other user — same data, same role restrictions — without needing that user's credentials.
272	
273	**How it works:**
274	
275	1. Admin clicks **Impersonate** in Admin → Users grid
276	2. `ImpersonationService.startImpersonation(userId)` calls `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId`
277	3. Backend issues a 2-hour RS256 JWT signed as the target user, with an extra `impersonated_by` claim carrying the admin's `user_id` for audit
278	4. Frontend saves the admin's current token as `admin_token` in `localStorage` and replaces `token` with the impersonation token
279	5. `UserAuthInterceptor` picks up the new token automatically — no interceptor changes needed
280	6. All subsequent requests run as the impersonated user (backend reads identity from the JWT `Authorization` header)
281	
282	**ImpersonationService** (`src/app/helpers/services/impersonation.service.ts`):
283	- `isImpersonating$` — `BehaviorSubject<boolean>` (true when `admin_token` is present in localStorage)
284	- `startImpersonation(userId)` — calls backend, swaps tokens, hard-navigates to `/`
285	- `stopImpersonation()` — restores `admin_token` → `token`, clears `admin_token`, hard-navigates to `/`
286	- `getImpersonatedUser()` — decodes current token to return `{ name, role }` for the banner
287	
288	**Impersonation banner:** An orange bar rendered at the top of `ToolbarComponent` whenever `isImpersonating$` is true: "Viewing as **[Name]** ([role]) — Stop Impersonating". The Stop button calls `stopImpersonation()`.
289	
290	**Entry point:** Impersonate button column (pinned left) in the Manage Users AG Grid (`src/app/pages/manage_users/manage_users.component.ts`), visible only in the Users view (not the Factories view). The page is already admin-only; no additional role check is needed in the component.
291	
292	**Intentional:** `stopImpersonation()` uses `window.location.href = '/'` rather than `router.navigate` to force a full page reload. This clears all in-memory state loaded under the impersonated identity, avoiding stale data in open components.
293	
294	---
295	
296	## File storage (DigitalOcean Spaces)
297	
298	Item images, customer logos, and tech link files are stored in DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible). The browser uploads directly to Spaces — the backend is never in the file-transfer path.
299	
300	**Upload flow (customer logos — `customer.service.ts`):**
301	
302	```
303	1. POST /getPreSignedUrl { fileName, contentType }
304	      → backend returns signed PUT URL (15 min expiry, no ACL in signature)
305	
306	2. Browser PUTs file to signed URL with Content-Type header
307	      → object created in Spaces (private by default)
308	
309	3. POST /setObjectPublic { fileName }
310	      → backend calls putObjectAcl({ ACL: 'public-read' }) via direct authenticated API call
311	      → returns { publicUrl } from DO_PUBLIC_URL backend env var
312	
313	4. PATCH /api/customers/:id { customers_logo: publicUrl }
314	      → URL saved to DB
315	```
316	
317	**Intentional:** ACL is set in a separate `putObjectAcl` call after upload, not via the presigned URL. DigitalOcean Spaces silently ignores `x-amz-acl` headers on presigned PUTs — the object would upload successfully but stay private.
318	
319	**Older item/file uploads** (`image-upload.component.ts`, `file-upload-dialog.component.ts`, `upload-file.component.ts`) construct the public URL in the frontend from `configService.DIGITAL_OCEAN_PUBLIC_URL` (`assets/config.json`) and call `POST /saveTechLink/:id` separately. These use a different code path and still rely on the bucket having the correct public access policy.
320	
321	**No-image placeholders:** `NO_IMAGE_URL` and `NO_IMAGE_THUMBNAIL_URL` in `assets/config.json` point to CDN-served placeholder images and are used when an item has no uploaded image.
322	
323	---
324	
325	## Key design constraints
326	
327	1. **No SSR** — the app is a pure SPA served as static files by nginx. All routing is client-side; nginx's `try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html` handles direct URL access.
328	2. **No runtime env injection** — environment config (API URLs) is baked into the Angular bundle at build time. Changing the API target requires a rebuild.
329	3. **AG Grid Enterprise** — the license is bundled; `AllEnterpriseModule` is registered globally in `app.module.ts`.
330	4. **JWT in localStorage** — the token is not httpOnly; XSS mitigation relies on Angular's built-in template sanitization and strict CSP (configured in nginx).
331

content.text: Excellent! Let me check how reactive forms are used in a dialog component example:

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile the findings into a clear, comprehensive report.

---

## Architecture & Conventions Report: Designflow Frontend

### 1. **Tech Stack**

| Component | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| **Framework** | Angular 21.1.3 (latest Angular) |
| **Router** | Client-side routing (no SSR) |
| **Language** | TypeScript 5.9.3 |
| **State Management** | Component-level with RxJS Observables; no centralized store (no Redux/NgRx) |
| **Data Fetching** | HttpClient with custom services; no react-query/SWR (Angular-native Observables + interceptors) |
| **Styling** | Tailwind CSS 2.0.4 + Angular Material 21.1.3 + custom SCSS |
| **Component Library** | Angular Material + PrimeNG (theme) + custom components |
| **Data Grid** | AG Grid Enterprise 35.2.1 (mandatory; license key hardcoded in modules) |
| **Forms** | Reactive Forms (`ReactiveFormsModule`, `FormControl`) + Template-driven forms |
| **UI Layout** | Vex commercial template (committed into repo at `src/@vex/`, not a package) |

---

### 2. **Feature Module Structure** — Best Example: **Production Tracking** (`src/app/pages/prod_tracking/`)

**Folder layout:**
```
prod_tracking/
├── prod_tracking.module.ts              ← NgModule declaring component + importing dependencies
├── prod_tracking-routing.module.ts      ← Route definitions (lazy-loaded as child route)
├── prod_tracking.component.ts           ← Main master grid component (~3600 lines typical)
├── prod_tracking.component.html         ← Template (toolbar + ag-grid-angular)
├── prod_tracking.component.scss         ← Scoped styles
├── helpers/
│   └── equation.ts                      ← Injectable service with calculation logic
├── detail-cell-renderer/                ← AG Grid detail row renderer (master-detail pattern)
│   ├── detail-cell-renderer.component.ts
│   ├── detail-cell-renderer.component.html
│   └── detail-cell-renderer.component.scss
├── duty_rate_dialog/                    ← Dialog components for grid interactions
│   ├── duty_rate_dialog.component.ts
│   ├── duty_rate_dialog.component.html
│   └── duty_rate_dialog.component.scss
├── layout-dropdown/                     ← Shared UI (grid layout switcher)
├── uploadPicture/                       ← File upload dialog
├── datepicker/, autocomplete/, custom-dropdown/, numericEditor/, etc.
```

**Key files and their responsibilities:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `prod_tracking.module.ts` | Declares all components, imports Material modules, AG Grid, and provides custom services |
| `prod_tracking-routing.module.ts` | Routes: defines single empty path `''` (child route) |
| `prod_tracking.component.ts` | **Master grid logic**: AG Grid setup, column wiring, valueSetter orchestration, master-detail cell renderer binding |
| `helpers/equation.ts` | **@Injectable()**: Margin & price calculations (e.g., `setGenFobMargin()`, `getFobTotalCost()`); called from component valueSetters |
| `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` | Renders detail grid independently; manages its own gridApi and column defs |
| Dialog components | Opened via `MatDialog.open()` from component; return data via `dialogRef.close(data)` |

**Naming conventions:**
- Component classes: PascalCase (`ProdTrackingComponent`, `DutyRateDialogComponent`)
- Component selectors: kebab-case (`app-prod_tracking`, `duty_rate_dialog`)
- Files: `component-name.component.ts` (component), `component-name.component.html` (template), `component-name.component.scss` (styles)
- Services: PascalCase class with `@Injectable()` and descriptive name (`prodEquation`, `TrackingService`)
- Colids (AG Grid column identifiers): lowercase_underscore (`gen_fob_margin`, `duty_rate`)
- Field names (AG Grid): Can have spaces (`"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)

---

### 3. **AG Grid Usage Pattern** — Mandatory Reference

**Key example:** `src/app/pages/prod_tracking/` + `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/prod.tracking.detail.config.ts`

**Column definition factory pattern:**
```typescript
// From prod.tracking.detail.config.ts
const commonDefaults = {
  filter: 'agTextColumnFilter',
  floatingFilter: true,
  editable: false,
  sortable: true
};

const addColumn = (headerName, field, colId, width, overrides = {}) => ({
  headerName,
  field,
  colId,
  col_id: colId,  // ← MUST include both colId and col_id for backwards compat
  width,
  ...commonDefaults,
  ...overrides
});
```

**Master-detail setup:**
- Master grid: AG Grid with `detailCellRenderer` component specified
- Detail grid: `DetailCellRenderer` component owns its own gridApi and column defs
- Pattern used in both RFQ and Production Tracking (row grouping, server-side row model, pagination)

**Async valueSetters:**
- Must return `true` (success) or `false`/call `snackBar.open()` (validation failure)
- Wrapped in try-catch; exceptions cancel the edit
- Called from component's `onGridReady()` to wire up column definitions dynamically

**Column identity resolution:**
- Use `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)` to get the field name string
- Never hardcode field names like `"FOB Cost"` — always resolve via colId lookup
- This handles the gap between stable colIds and user-visible field names

**Grid options:**
- Row model: `'serverSide'` with pagination (not client-side row model)
- Status bar: agTotalAndFilteredRowCountComponent, agTotalRowCountComponent
- **Critical:** Do NOT add `aggFunc` to columns (see AGENTS.md §11 for why)

---

### 4. **Backend Communication** — API Client Setup

**HTTP architecture:**
```
Browser → Angular HttpClient
         ↓
      3-tier interceptor stack (in `app.module.ts` provider order):
         1. UserAuthInterceptor       (adds Authorization: Bearer token)
         2. LoaderInterceptor         (shows global spinner for X-Enable-Loader: true)
         3. HttpErrorInterceptor      (catches errors, shows snackbar via api-error.ts)
         ↓
      Backend REST API at environment.url
```

**API client pattern** (`src/app/helpers/services/main.service.ts`):
```typescript
@Injectable()
export class itemService {
  serverAddress = environment.url;  // ← Baked at build time (no runtime injection)
  
  setUserAuth(): HttpHeaders {
    const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
    return token ? new HttpHeaders().set('Authorization', token) : new HttpHeaders();
  }
  
  setUserAuthWithLoader(): HttpHeaders {
    const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
    return token ? new HttpHeaders().set('Authorization', token) : new HttpHeaders()
      .set('X-Enable-Loader', 'true');  // ← Triggers global loader spinner
  }
  
  getGridHeader(data) {
    return this.http.post(this.serverAddress + '/getRFQViewlayout', data, 
      { headers: this.setUserAuth() });
  }
}
```

**Base URL config** (`src/environments/environment.ts`):
```typescript
export const environment = {
  production: false,
  url: 'http://localhost:5004/api/core',           // ← Main CRUD
  tracking: 'http://localhost:5004/api/tracking',  // ← Tracking-specific
  item_master: 'http://localhost:5004/api/item_master',
  data_sync: 'http://localhost:5004/api/data_sync',
  // Azure SSO
  azureClientId: '8a8695bd-...',
  azureTenantId: '1caeb1c0-...',
};
```

**Service pattern for tracking** (`src/app/helpers/services/tracking.service.ts`):
- Separate service with its own base URL (`environment.tracking`)
- Same auth header pattern; methods return Observables
- Pagination: pass `{ startRow, endRow, filterModel }` as HttpParams

---

### 5. **Forms & File Uploads**

**Forms:** Reactive Forms with `FormControl` / `FormBuilder`
```typescript
// Example from duty_rate_dialog.component.ts:
onSave(percent: string, dollar: string) {
  const percentVal = evaluateInput(percent);  // Math expression parser
  const dollarVal = dollar.trim() ? evaluateInput(dollar) : 0;
  this.dialogRef.close({ duty_rate: rate.toFixed(2), ... });
}
```

**File uploads to DigitalOcean Spaces** (`src/app/pages/prod_tracking/uploadPicture/`):
```typescript
export class uploadPictureComponent {
  async upload() {
    for (const file of this.uploadedFiles) {
      const imageName = `images-${new Date().getTime()}-${file.name.toLowerCase()}`;
      await this.helperService.saveToDigitalOceanAndDB(imageName, file.type, file);
      this.imageUrls.FullSizePicture = `${doUrl}${imageName}`;
      
      // Resize & upload thumbnail
      this.helperService.resizeImage(file, thumbName).then(async (thumbFile) => {
        await this.helperService.saveToDigitalOceanAndDB(thumbName, file.type, thumbFile);
        this.imageUrls.Thumbnail = `${doUrl}${thumbName}`;
      });
    }
  }
  
  onSave() {
    this.dialogRef.close({ event: 'Save', data: this.imageUrls });
  }
}
```

**Upload flow:**
1. POST `/getPreSignedUrl` → get signed S3 URL (15 min expiry)
2. Browser PUT file directly to Spaces
3. POST `/setObjectPublic` → set ACL to public-read
4. Save publicUrl to DB

**Other upload examples:**
- `src/app/components/file-upload/file-upload-dialog.component.ts`
- `src/app/components/image-upload/image-upload.component.ts`
- `src/app/pages/itemDetail/image-upload/`

---

### 6. **Routing & Navigation**

**Route registration** (`src/app/app-routing.module.ts`):
```typescript
{
  path: 'apps/prod-tracking',
  data: { role: 'admin' },
  canActivate: [AuthGuard],
  loadChildren: () => import('./pages/prod_tracking/prod_tracking.module')
    .then(m => m.ProdTrackingModule)
},
```

**Menu registration** (`src/app/app.component.ts`):
```typescript
this.navigationService.items = [
  {
    type: 'dropdown',
    label: 'Tracking',
    role: ['admin', 'production', 'sourcing_manager', 'designer'],
    route: '',
    icon: 'pi pi-qrcode',
    children: [
      {
        type: 'link',
        label: 'Licensing',
        route: '/apps/licensing-tracking',
        icon: 'badge'
      },
      {
        type: 'link',
        label: 'Production',
        route: '/apps/prod-tracking',
        icon: 'pi pi-server'
      }
    ]
  }
];
```

**Guards:**
- `AuthGuard` (checks JWT valid + not expired)
- `RoleGuard` (checks JWT role against route `data.role` array)

**Key points:**
- Routes are lazy-loaded via `loadChildren`
- Role-based visibility in nav via `hidden` flags set per role
- Single-URL navigation support via `onSameUrlNavigation: 'reload'` in RouterModule config

---

### 7. **Code Conventions from CLAUDE.md & Linting**

**Commit style:**
```
type(scope): short description

Optional body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Types: `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`
